agent_raw: The observed 16% increase in recovered balance per account should not be treated as strong evidence that the model caused better recoveries. The comparison spans two different calendar seasons — the post-deployment period falls in tax-refund season, when delinquent borrowers commonly receive liquidity from refunds and pay voluntarily at higher rates. This seasonal effect is independent of the model's ranking quality. Simultaneously, the lender changed dialer vendors, improving contactability and altering which accounts were even reachable. Manager overrides for hardship and promised-to-pay accounts further blurred the treatment assignment. None of these confounds are controlled for, and there is no same-period control group to serve as a counterfactual. The combined system may be working well, but the model's specific contribution cannot be isolated from this evaluation. A concurrent champion-challenger design is needed.

empirical_test.condition: Champion-challenger randomized queue test in the same calendar period
empirical_test.supports_critique_if: Model-ranked accounts show no material recovery difference from legacy-ranked accounts under controlled dialer and override conditions
empirical_test.supports_defense_if: Model-ranked accounts deliver materially higher recoveries than legacy-ranked accounts in a controlled concurrent test
empirical_test.ambiguous_if: Some gain persists but concentrated in newly-reachable or refund-season accounts
